utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as required: (1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes both physical and mental health descriptions and a monthly_cost; thus fully specified. (2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. It has a plan name, monthly_cost, and a description indicating home coverage; contents are not explicitly stated but the format asks for home/contents with costs—description implies home coverage, not explicit contents details; however, since it is framed as home coverage with concierge claim service and luxury item coverage, it reasonably covers property and cost; consider sufficiently specified. (3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. Provided with plan and monthly_cost; sufficient. (4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan lists additional services as integrated within the above plans but provides no separate costs; this is not fully specified with costs. (5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. They provide a monthly total and breakdown by category but no annual cost; thus not fully specified as it lacks annual breakdown.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three items by name: EliteCare Premium Enhanced Package for health (which aligns with EliteCare Premium Plan under both physical and mental health in the ground truth), EliteHome Premium Coverage for property, and EliteTravel Premium Plan for travel. We map each to its corresponding rating from the ground-truth tables.
